title: ranunculus strongylus
intro: Ranunculus strongylus, also known as Lesser Celandine, is a perennial plant species native to Europe and North America, characterized by its yellow flowers and creeping habit. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which make it effective in treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The health benefits of Ranunculus strongylus include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems such as bronchitis and coughs. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and salves, which are often used topically to treat skin conditions and wounds.
